hints.text: Long white plate + blue strip; commercial = **yellow**; **"Parrot" plates** (yellow section on the left) are unique to Denmark
Generally **flat** with wide open fields; spacious suburbs, denser urban cores
All-white lines, **double middle common**; **outer lines made of small white squares** — a great Denmark tell
Very unique **direction signs**: low to the ground, **pipe-framed**, red/white or blue/white with the arrow in the background
Street names end **-vej**; blue street sign, white text
Unique **white bollard: yellow reflector + dark-orange stripe** (also a green variant); Belgium's lacks the orange stripe
Danish letters **Æ Ø Å** (Norwegian same; Swedish uses Å Ä Ö instead)
Architecture **red brick**, orange/brown/black tiled roofs; **thatched roofs** rural; timber-frame on historical buildings
Signpost with the **pipe frame bent around the sign** = unique to Denmark
**Square B-type guardrails, no reflectors** — only a few European countries use this
Pedestrian sign **5 stripes**, first & fifth **touch the triangle edge**
Red-and-white **chevrons**, often low to the ground; unique **town-entry sign** (red slash when leaving)
Street-level poles **rare** → mostly big **overhead power lines** near highways; bicycle paths common
**PostNord** postal boxes; gas brands Ingo / OK / Uno-X / YX (each shared with one neighbour)
Road numbering runs **east→west**; roads 2–5 grouped by region; **Bornholm only has 1-roads**
**6 regional bus companies**, each with its own colour scheme + bus-stop sign
**Bornholm**: green/red/white bus stops + green bicycle signs
**Anholt** island: only **Gen2**, gloomy overcast, sandy dirt on the roadside
